summary: probe full findings plus full opp threats

findings.claim: Winners absorb complexity behind a simple surface. Stripe seven lines of code hid fraud engines, bank relationships, compliance.
A one-line promise (Accept payments in seven lines of code) is the manifesto and positioning, not just a tagline.
A step-by-step interview makes a huge domain doable. TurboTax encodes 70000+ pages of tax law plus 45 states.
Ease of setup beats feature breadth. Shopify won SMBs over Magento on launch-in-hours-no-code.
A recombinable block plus one uniform UI lets non-developers build tools. Notion LEGO-block model is its moat.
Winning AI-agent packaging is the hire-an-AI-employee frame: a role-based worker hired in 5 minutes.
Visual builders package agent complexity; an agent now takes 15-60 minutes and domain experts build them.
Per-seat pricing breaks in the agent era; outcome models align price to value.
Three tiers with an anchored middle is most legible; more than 3-4 tiers causes paralysis.
Adoption hinges on a real aha moment in the first session. Cutting time-to-value 30% lifts trial-to-paid 15-25%.
Onboarding should be short: tours over four steps have below-average completion.
Word-of-mouth compounds when the simple surface delights one dense community first.

opportunities: Lead with ONE legible promise; keep tribunal/FORGE/posture machinery invisible.
Frame as a hired AI employee/team with named roles (your AI financial analyst, your AI Power Platform engineer).
Make the machinery VISIBLE as trust signals: posture dashboard, tribunal verdict log, audit dashboards in the first 5 minutes.
Engineer a first-run aha: produce ONE real artifact (dashboard, FORGE plan, posture applied), not a tutorial.
Ship opinionated defaults (strict/balanced/exploratory) so non-developers never author a rule by hand.
Present plugins/agents as recombinable LEGO-style blocks behind one uniform dashboard.
Price as three tiers with an anchored middle (fits 25-50k engagements); consider outcome-framed items over per-seat.
Seed one dense community first (Power Platform / financial-analyst circles).
Keep guided setup to four or fewer steps.
threats: A slash-command-first/CLI-ritual surface is the trap; dashboards-over-slash-commands must be enforced product-wide.
Exposing the sophistication as the PITCH scares non-developers and reads as developer-tooling.
A setup/tutorial first session drives week-one churn.
Per-seat pricing mirroring under-the-hood complexity is illegible; loses to outcome/tier pricing.
All-in-one breadth (26 plugins) reads as overwhelming unless wrapped in one surface and one promise.
Incumbents (Lindy, Relevance AI) own the simple non-developer surface; RavenClaude audit edge must be legible to non-developers.
